title: Delicate Malty Ethiopian Flax Dulet
description: A malty, flaky Ethiopian dish featuring flax eggs and papillote, perfect for a low-carb hangover cure. This dulet recipe combines traditional Ethiopian flavors with modern low-carb ingredients.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, mix together the ground flaxseed and water to create a flax egg, stirring well to combine.
Let the flax egg sit for 5 minutes to thicken, then stir in the malted barley flour, paprika, onion powder, garlic powder, salt, and black pepper.
Heat the coconut oil in a large skillet over medium heat, then pour in the flax egg mixture and scramble until it is just set.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake for 10 minutes, or until the edges are lightly golden.
Remove the skillet from the oven and stir in the chopped scallions.
Serve the dulet hot, garnished with additional scallions if desired.
To cook in papillote, place a portion of the dulet mixture on a piece of parchment paper, fold the paper to create a packet, and bake for an additional 5 minutes.
